    # - Adaptive threshold: only accept moves that reduce max KVPR by > 0.5% of current gap
    # - Prioritize moving models with high load-to-size ratio from high-KVPR GPUs
    # - Try multiple swaps per iteration
    # - Introduce small random perturbations if stuck
    
    max_iterations = 150
    convergence_threshold = 1e-6
    improvement_count = 0
    
    for iter_idx in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        current_kvprs = [compute_kvpr(gpu_loads[g], gpu_sizes[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)]
        max_kvpr = max(current_kvprs)
        bottleneck_gpu = current_kvprs.index(max_kvpr)
        
        # Identify target GPUs (those with KVPR significantly lower than bottleneck)
        target_gpus = [g for g in range(gpu_num) if g != bottleneck_gpu and current_kvprs[g] < max_kvpr - convergence_threshold]
        target_gpus.sort(key=lambda g: (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g], -current_kvprs[g]), reverse=True)
        
        if not target_gpus:
            break  # No improvement possible
            
        improvement_found = False
        best_improvement = 0
        
        # Try moving models from bottleneck to target GPUs
        for target_gpu in target_gpus:
            # Sort models on bottleneck by impact: prioritize high load/size ratio
            # to reduce bottleneck KVPR most efficiently
            bottleneck_models = placement[bottleneck_gpu][:]
            bottleneck_models.sort(key=lambda i: model_loads[i] / max(1, model_sizes[i]), reverse=True)
            
            for m_idx in bottleneck_models:
                # Check memory constraint
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + model_sizes[m_idx] > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Simulate move
                new_load_b = gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] - model_loads[m_idx]
                new_size_b = gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] - model_sizes[m_idx]
                new_kvpr_b = compute_kvpr(new_load_b, new_size_b)
                
                new_load_t = gpu_loads[target_gpu] + model_loads[m_idx]
                new_size_t = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + model_sizes[m_idx]
                new_kvpr_t = compute_kvpr(new_load_t, new_size_t)
                
                # New max KVPR after move
                other_max = max(v for g, v in enumerate(current_kvprs) if g not in [bottleneck_gpu, target_gpu])
                new_max = max(new_kvpr_b, new_kvpr_t, other_max)
                
                # Adaptive threshold: only accept if improvement is significant
                improvement = max_kvpr - new_max
                if improvement > convergence_threshold * 10:  # At least 10x threshold improvement
                    # Execute move
                    placement[bottleneck_gpu].remove(m_idx)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(m_idx)
                    
                    gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] = new_load_b
                    gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] = new_size_b
                    gpu_loads[target_gpu] = new_load_t
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_size_t
                    
                    improvement_found = True
                    best_improvement = max(best_improvement, improvement)
                    break
            
            if improvement_found:
                break
        
        # If no move found, try swapping with another GPU
        if not improvement_found:
            # Try swapping with the GPU that has the lowest KVPR and most free space
            other_gpu_candidates = [g for g in range(gpu_num) if g != bottleneck_gpu]
            other_gpu_candidates.sort(key=lambda g: (current_kvprs[g], -(GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])))
            
            for other_gpu in other_gpu_candidates:
                # Try swapping pairs of models
                for m1 in placement[bottleneck_gpu]:
                    for m2 in placement[other_gpu]:
                        # Check memory constraints for swap
                        if (gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] - model_sizes[m1] + model_sizes[m2] > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[other_gpu] - model_sizes[m2] + model_sizes[m1] > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Simulate swap
                        new_load_b = gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] - model_loads[m1] + model_loads[m2]
                        new_size_b = gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] - model_sizes[m1] + model_sizes[m2]
                        new_kvpr_b = compute_kvpr(new_load_b, new_size_b)
                        
                        new_load_o = gpu_loads[other_gpu] - model_loads[m2] + model_loads[m1]
                        new_size_o = gpu_sizes[other_gpu] - model_sizes[m2] + model_sizes[m1]
                        new_kvpr_o = compute_kvpr(new_load_o, new_size_o)
                        
                        # New max KVPR
                        other_max = max(v for g, v in enumerate(current_kvprs) if g not in [bottleneck_gpu, other_gpu])
                        new_max = max(new_kvpr_b, new_kvpr_o, other_max)
                        
                        improvement = max_kvpr - new_max
                        if improvement > convergence_threshold * 10:
                            # Execute swap
                            placement[bottleneck_gpu].remove(m1)
                            placement[bottleneck_gpu].append(m2)
                            placement[other_gpu].remove(m2)
                            placement[other_gpu].append(m1)
                            
                            gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] = new_load_b
                            gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] = new_size_b
                            gpu_loads[other_gpu] = new_load_o
                            gpu_sizes[other_gpu] = new_size_o
                            
                            improvement_found = True
                            best_improvement = max(best_improvement, improvement)
                            break
                    if improvement_found:
                        break
                if improvement_found:
                    break
        
        # If no improvement in multiple iterations, introduce small random shuffle
        if not improvement_found:
            improvement_count += 1
            if improvement_count >= 5:
                # Random shuffle of models across GPUs to escape local minima
                all_models = []
                for g in range(gpu_num):
                    all_models.extend(placement[g])
                    placement[g].clear()
                
                # Reassign randomly but maintain memory constraint
                for idx in all_models:
                    best_gpu = -1
                    best_score = float('inf')
                    for g in range(gpu_num):
                        if gpu_sizes[g] + model_sizes[idx] > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                            continue
                        new_load = gpu_loads[g] + model_loads[idx]
                        new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + model_sizes[idx]
                        new_kvpr = compute_kvpr(new_load, new_size)
                        penalty = math.log(1.0 + 10 * (new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE)) * 0.005
                        score = new_kvpr + penalty
                        if score < best_score:
                            best_score = score
                            best_gpu = g
                    if best_gpu != -1:
                        placement[best_gpu].append(idx)
                        gpu_loads[best_gpu] += model_loads[idx]
                        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += model_sizes[idx]
                improvement_count = 0
        else:
            improvement_count = 0  # Reset on improvement
        
        if not improvement_found and best_improvement < convergence_threshold:
            break  # Converged
    
    # Convert indices back to Model objects
    final_result = {}
    for gpu_id, model_indices in placement.items():
        final_result[gpu_id] = [models[i] for i in model_indices]
    
    return final_result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import math
import random
import heapq

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute metrics for all models
    model_loads = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_sizes = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    def compute_kvpr(load: float, size: float) -> float:
        """Compute KVPR for a GPU with given load and size."""
        if size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-8:
            return float('inf')
        return load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - size)
    
    def evaluate_placement(placement: Dict[int, List[int]]) -> float:
        """Evaluate max KVPR for a placement (using model indices)."""
        max_kvpr = 0.0
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_id not in placement or not placement[gpu_id]:
                continue
            load = sum(model_loads[i] for i in placement[gpu_id])
            size = sum(model_sizes[i] for i in placement[gpu_id])
            kvpr = compute_kvpr(load, size)
            if kvpr > max_kvpr:
                max_kvpr = kvpr
        return max_kvpr
    
    def evaluate_placement_with_loads(placement: Dict[int, List[int]], gpu_loads: List[float], gpu_sizes: List[float]) -> float:
        """Evaluate max KVPR using precomputed GPU states."""
        max_kvpr = 0.0
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            kvpr = compute_kvpr(gpu_loads[g], gpu_sizes[g])
            if kvpr > max_kvpr:
                max_kvpr = kvpr
        return max_kvpr
    
    # STRATEGY 1: Binary Search on Target KVPR with Greedy Packing
    # Enhanced with tighter thresholds and smarter model ordering
    
    def can_pack_with_threshold(target_kvpr: float) -> tuple[bool, Dict[int, List[int]]]:
        """
        Check if we can pack all models such that no GPU exceeds target_kvpr.
        Uses size-descending + load-descending with memory-aware placement.
        """
        # Sort models by size descending, then by load descending (critical for bin packing)
        sorted_indices = sorted(range(n_models), key=lambda i: (model_sizes[i], model_loads[i]), reverse=True)
        
        placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        gpu_loads = [0.0] * gpu_num
        gpu_sizes = [0.0] * gpu_num
        
        # For each model, find the best GPU that keeps it under target
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            load = model_loads[idx]
            size = model_sizes[idx]
            
            # Find all feasible GPUs: satisfy memory AND KVPR constraint
            feasible_gpus = []
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                # Memory constraint
                if gpu_sizes[g] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-8:
                    continue
                
                # KVPR constraint
                new_load = gpu_loads[g] + load
                new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + size
                new_kvpr = compute_kvpr(new_load, new_size)
                
                if new_kvpr <= target_kvpr:
                    # Score: prioritize GPUs with lower current KVPR and better space utilization
                    # Penalize high current KVPR, reward unused space
                    space_util = (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g]) / GPU_MEM_SIZE
                    score = new_kvpr + 0.1 * (1 - space_util)  # Favor unused space
                    feasible_gpus.append((score, g))
            
            if not feasible_gpus:
                return False, None
            
            # Choose the GPU with the lowest score (best balance of KVPR and space)
            feasible_gpus.sort()
            best_gpu = feasible_gpus[0][1]
            
            # Place model
            placement[best_gpu].append(idx)
            gpu_loads[best_gpu] += load
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        
        return True, placement
    
    # Calculate theoretical bounds for binary search
    total_load = sum(model_loads)
    total_size = sum(model_sizes)
    
    # Lower bound: worst-case single GPU (but not if impossible)
    min_possible_kvpr = compute_kvpr(total_load, total_size) if total_size < GPU_MEM_SIZE else float('inf')
    
    # Upper bound: average across GPUs
    avg_load = total_load / gpu_num
    avg_size = total_size / gpu_num
    max_possible_kvpr = compute_kvpr(avg_load, avg_size) if avg_size < GPU_MEM_SIZE else float('inf')
    
    # If total size exceeds capacity, fallback to greedy
    if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE * gpu_num:
        # Use best-fit decreasing with size only
        sorted_indices = sorted(range(n_models), key=lambda i: model_sizes[i], reverse=True)
        placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            # Choose GPU with most remaining space
            best_gpu = max(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_sizes[i] for i in placement[g]))
            placement[best_gpu].append(idx)
        return {gpu_id: [models[i] for i in placement[gpu_id]] for gpu_id in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Binary search with tighter bounds and adaptive precision
    low = max(0.0, min_possible_kvpr - 0.05)
    high = max(low + 0.005, max_possible_kvpr + 0.5)
    
    best_feasible_placement = None
    best_threshold = high
    
    # Adaptive iteration count based on model count
    max_iter = 35 + max(0, n_models - 10)  # More models → more precision
    
    for _ in range(max_iter):
        mid = (low + high) / 2.0
        feasible, placement = can_pack_with_threshold(mid)
        
        if feasible:
            best_feasible_placement = placement
            best_threshold = mid
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
        
        # Early termination on small range
        if high - low < 1e-5:
            break
    
    # Fallback: use last known feasible placement or relax threshold
    if best_feasible_placement is None:
        # Try with a more relaxed threshold
        _, best_feasible_placement = can_pack_with_threshold(max_possible_kvpr * 1.5)
        if best_feasible_placement is None:
            # Last resort: round-robin
            best_feasible_placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
            for i, model in enumerate(models):
                best_feasible_placement[i % gpu_num].append(i)
    
    # STRATEGY 2: Post-processing refinement with adaptive local search
    placement = best_feasible_placement
    gpu_loads = [sum(model_loads[i] for i in placement[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)]
    gpu_sizes = [sum(model_sizes[i] for i in placement[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)]
    
    current_score = evaluate_placement_with_loads(placement, gpu_loads, gpu_sizes)
    
    # Adaptive refinement with dynamic stopping
    max_refinement_iter = 2000
    improvement_threshold = 1e-6
    patience = 100
    
    last_improvement = 0
    for iter_count in range(max_refinement_iter):
        kvprs = [compute_kvpr(gpu_loads[g], gpu_sizes[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)]
        max_gpu = max(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: kvprs[g])
        min_gpu = min(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: kvprs[g])
        
        # If balanced, stop
        if kvprs[max_gpu] - kvprs[min_gpu] < improvement_threshold:
            break
        
        moved = False
        
        # Strategy A: Move best candidate from max to min
        if placement[max_gpu]:
            # Sort by impact: high load, small size, high KVPR increase if left
            candidates = sorted(
                placement[max_gpu],
                key=lambda i: (model_loads[i], -model_sizes[i], -kvprs[max_gpu]),
                reverse=True
            )
            
            for model_idx in candidates:
                if gpu_sizes[min_gpu] + model_sizes[model_idx] > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-8:
                    continue
                
                new_load_max = gpu_loads[max_gpu] - model_loads[model_idx]
                new_size_max = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - model_sizes[model_idx]
                new_kvpr_max = compute_kvpr(new_load_max, new_size_max)
                
                new_load_min = gpu_loads[min_gpu] + model_loads[model_idx]
                new_size_min = gpu_sizes[min_gpu] + model_sizes[model_idx]
                new_kvpr_min = compute_kvpr(new_load_min, new_size_min)
                
                new_max_kvpr = max(new_kvpr_max, new_kvpr_min)
                old_max_kvpr = kvprs[max_gpu]
                
                if new_max_kvpr < old_max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                    # Apply move
                    placement[max_gpu].remove(model_idx)
                    placement[min_gpu].append(model_idx)
                    
                    gpu_loads[max_gpu] = new_load_max
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_size_max
                    gpu_loads[min_gpu] = new_load_min
                    gpu_sizes[min_gpu] = new_size_min
                    
                    moved = True
                    last_improvement = iter_count
                    break
        
        # Strategy B: Try swaps with second-highest KVPR GPU
        if not moved:
            other_gpus = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: kvprs[g], reverse=True)
            for other_gpu in other_gpus:
                if other_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[max_gpu] or not placement[other_gpu]:
                    continue
                
                # Try swapping pairs
                for m1 in placement[max_gpu]:
                    for m2 in placement[other_gpu]:
                        # Memory feasibility
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - model_sizes[m1] + model_sizes[m2] > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-8 or
                            gpu_sizes[other_gpu] - model_sizes[m2] + model_sizes[m1] > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-8):
                            continue
                        
                        new_load1 = gpu_loads[max_gpu] - model_loads[m1] + model_loads[m2]
                        new_size1 = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - model_sizes[m1] + model_sizes[m2]
                        new_kvpr1 = compute_kvpr(new_load1, new_size1)
                        
                        new_load2 = gpu_loads[other_gpu] - model_loads[m2] + model_loads[m1]
                        new_size2 = gpu_sizes[other_gpu] - model_sizes[m2] + model_sizes[m1]
                        new_kvpr2 = compute_kvpr(new_load2, new_size2)
                        
                        current_max = max(kvprs[max_gpu], kvprs[other_gpu])
                        new_max = max(new_kvpr1, new_kvpr2)
                        
                        if new_max < current_max - improvement_threshold:
                            # Apply swap
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(m1)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(m2)
                            placement[other_gpu].remove(m2)
                            placement[other_gpu].append(m1)
                            
                            gpu_loads[max_gpu] = new_load1
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_size1
                            gpu_loads[other_gpu] = new_load2
                            gpu_sizes[other_gpu] = new_size2
                            
                            moved = True
                            last_improvement = iter_count
                            break
                    if moved:
                        break
                if moved:
                    break
        
        # Early termination if no improvement in patience rounds
        if not moved and iter_count - last_improvement > patience:
            break
    
    # Convert indices to Model objects
    result = {}
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        result[gpu_id] = [models[i] for i in placement[gpu_id]]
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import torch
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Convert to tensors
    n_models = len(models)
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_loads = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Sort by load density (load per GB) descending - highest impact models first
    load_density = model_loads / (model_sizes + 1e-9)
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(load_density, descending=True)
    model_sizes = model_sizes[sorted_indices]
    model_loads = model_loads[sorted_indices]
    
    # Phase 1: Fast Iterative Greedy with Enhanced Dynamic Scoring
    # Use a refined heuristic that emphasizes load balancing and avoids premature saturation
    
    def compute_placement_with_heuristic(heuristic_variant=0):
        """Construct placement using adaptive greedy heuristic with improved scoring."""
        gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_loads = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        assignment = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
        
        for idx in range(n_models):
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            load = model_loads[idx].item()
            
            # Find valid GPUs
            valid_mask = (gpu_sizes + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6)
            
            if not valid_mask.any():
                # Fallback: place on GPU with largest remaining space, even if marginal
                available_space = GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes
                target_gpu = torch.argmax(available_space).item()
                # Force fit if possible
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    # If still too big, use least loaded GPU (last resort)
                    target_gpu = torch.argmin(gpu_sizes).item()
            else:
                valid_gpus = torch.where(valid_mask)[0]
                
                # Calculate adaptive scores
                scores = []
                for g in valid_gpus:
                    new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + size
                    new_load = gpu_loads[g] + load
                    
                    # KVPR component
                    remaining = GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size
                    kvpr = new_load / (remaining + 1e-9)
                    
                    # Load balancing: prefer GPUs closer to average load
                    total_load = gpu_loads.sum() + load
                    avg_load = total_load / gpu_num
                    load_imbalance = abs((gpu_loads[g] + load) - avg_load)
                    
                    # Memory utilization: prefer filling moderately full GPUs (avoid fragmentation)
                    utilization = new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE
                    space_ratio = 1.0 - utilization
                    
                    # Adjusted scoring with tuned weights
                    if heuristic_variant == 0:
                        # Prioritize KVPR minimization
                        score = kvpr * 2.0 + load_imbalance * 0.3
                    elif heuristic_variant == 1:
                        # Balanced: KVPR + imbalance + utilization bias
                        score = kvpr * 2.5 + load_imbalance * 0.2 + space_ratio * 0.5
                    elif heuristic_variant == 2:
                        # Aggressive space use with KVPR control
                        score = kvpr * 1.5 + (1.0 - utilization) * 1.5
                    
                    scores.append(score)
                
                best_idx = torch.argmin(torch.tensor(scores)).item()
                target_gpu = valid_gpus[best_idx].item()
            
            assignment[target_gpu].append(idx)
            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += size
            gpu_loads[target_gpu] += load
        
        # Compute final KVPRs
        kvprs = gpu_loads / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes)
        kvprs[torch.isinf(kvprs)] = 0
        max_kvpr = kvprs.max().item()
        
        return assignment, max_kvpr, kvprs
    
    # Generate 3 initial solutions with different heuristics
    best_assignment = None
    best_max_kvpr = float('inf')
    best_kvprs = None
    
    for h_variant in range(3):
        assignment, max_kvpr, kvprs = compute_placement_with_heuristic(h_variant)
        if max_kvpr < best_max_kvpr:
            best_max_kvpr = max_kvpr
            best_assignment = assignment
            best_kvprs = kvprs
    
    # Phase 2: Gradient-Inspired Local Search with Adaptive Step Size
    # Improve convergence with dynamic step size and early termination
    
    current_assignment = [list(gpu) for gpu in best_assignment]
    current_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    current_loads = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        for idx in current_assignment[g]:
            current_sizes[g] += model_sizes[idx]
            current_loads[g] += model_loads[idx]
    
    current_kvprs = current_loads / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - current_sizes)
    current_kvprs[torch.isinf(current_kvprs)] = 0
    
    # Adaptive iteration count and step size
    max_iter = 1200
    min_improvement_threshold = 1e-7  # Lower threshold for later stages
    decay_rate = 0.95  # Reduce step size over time
    
    for iteration in range(max_iter):
        # Identify source (high pressure) and destination (low pressure)
        source_gpu = torch.argmax(current_kvprs).item()
        dest_gpu = torch.argmin(current_kvprs).item()
        
        if source_gpu == dest_gpu:
            break
        
        source_kvpr = current_kvprs[source_gpu].item()
        dest_kvpr = current_kvprs[dest_gpu].item()
        
        # Early termination if imbalance is negligible
        if source_kvpr - dest_kvpr < 1e-6:
            break
        
        # Adaptive step size based on iteration
        step_size = 1.0 * (decay_rate ** iteration)
        
        # Strategy: Find best model to move, prioritizing high-impact moves
        best_move = None
        best_improvement = 0
        
        for i, idx in enumerate(current_assignment[source_gpu]):
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            load = model_loads[idx].item()
            
            # Check feasibility
            if current_sizes[dest_gpu] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
                continue
            
            # Compute new KVPRs
            new_source_size = current_sizes[source_gpu] - size
            new_source_load = current_loads[source_gpu] - load
            new_dest_size = current_sizes[dest_gpu] + size
            new_dest_load = current_loads[dest_gpu] + load
            
            new_source_kvpr = new_source_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_source_size) if new_source_size > 1e-6 else 0
            new_dest_kvpr = new_dest_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_dest_size)
            
            # Compute new max KVPR
            new_max = max(new_source_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr)
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if g != source_gpu and g != dest_gpu:
                    new_max = max(new_max, current_kvprs[g].item())
            
            improvement = current_kvprs.max().item() - new_max
            
            # Prioritize moves that:
            # 1. Reduce max KVPR significantly
            # 2. Move high-load-density models
            # 3. Balance the two GPUs
            load_density = load / (size + 1e-9)
            priority = improvement * 15 + load_density * 0.2 + (source_kvpr - dest_kvpr) * 0.1
            
            if priority > best_improvement:
                best_improvement = priority
                best_move = (i, idx, new_source_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr, 
                           new_source_size, new_source_load, new_dest_size, new_dest_load)
        
        if best_move is None:
            # Try swaps if moves fail
            best_swap = None
            best_swap_improvement = 0
            
            for g1 in range(gpu_num):
                for g2 in range(g1 + 1, gpu_num):
                    if not current_assignment[g1] or not current_assignment[g2]:
                        continue
                    
                    for idx1 in current_assignment[g1]:
                        for idx2 in current_assignment[g2]:
                            size1 = model_sizes[idx1].item()
                            load1 = model_loads[idx1].item()
                            size2 = model_sizes[idx2].item()
                            load2 = model_loads[idx2].item()
                            
                            # Check feasibility
                            if (current_sizes[g1] - size1 + size2 > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6 or
                                current_sizes[g2] - size2 + size1 > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6):
                                continue
                            
                            # Compute new KVPRs
                            new_size1 = current_sizes[g1] - size1 + size2
                            new_load1 = current_loads[g1] - load1 + load2
                            new_size2 = current_sizes[g2] - size2 + size1
                            new_load2 = current_loads[g2] - load2 + load1
                            
                            new_kvpr1 = new_load1 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size1)
                            new_kvpr2 = new_load2 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size2)
                            
                            new_max = max(new_kvpr1, new_kvpr2)
                            for g in range(gpu_num):
                                if g != g1 and g != g2:
                                    new_max = max(new_max, current_kvprs[g].item())
                            
                            improvement = current_kvprs.max().item() - new_max
                            
                            if improvement > best_swap_improvement:
                                best_swap_improvement = improvement
                                best_swap = (g1, g2, idx1, idx2, new_size1, new_load1, new_size2, new_load2)
            
            if best_swap is None or best_swap_improvement <= 0:
                break
            
            # Execute swap
            g1, g2, idx1, idx2, new_size1, new_load1, new_size2, new_load2 = best_swap
            
            current_assignment[g1].remove(idx1)
            current_assignment[g1].append(idx2)
            current_assignment[g2].remove(idx2)
            current_assignment[g2].append(idx1)
            
            current_sizes[g1] = new_size1
            current_loads[g1] = new_load1
            current_sizes[g2] = new_size2
            current_loads[g2] = new_load2
            
            current_kvprs[g1] = new_load1 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size1)
            current_kvprs[g2] = new_load2 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size2)
            
        else:
            # Execute move
            i, idx, new_source_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr, \
            new_source_size, new_source_load, new_dest_size, new_dest_load = best_move
            
            current_assignment[source_gpu].pop(i)
            current_assignment[dest_gpu].append(idx)
            
            current_sizes[source_gpu] = new_source_size
            current_loads[source_gpu] = new_source_load
            current_sizes[dest_gpu] = new_dest_size
            current_loads[dest_gpu] = new_dest_load
            
            current_kvprs[source_gpu] = new_source_kvpr
            current_kvprs[dest_gpu] = new_dest_kvpr
    
    # Phase 3: Refinement with Load-Density Swaps and Early Exit
    # Focus on high-impact models in high-pressure GPUs, with tighter exit condition
    
    for _ in range(150):
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(current_kvprs).item()
        max_kvpr = current_kvprs[max_gpu].item()
        
        if max_kvpr < 1e-7:
            break
        
        # Find models in max_gpu sorted by load density
        models_in_max = []
        for i, idx in enumerate(current_assignment[max_gpu]):
            load = model_loads[idx].item()
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            models_in_max.append((load / (size + 1e-9), i, idx, load, size))
        
        models_in_max.sort(reverse=True)  # Highest load density first
        
        moved = False
        
        for _, i, idx, load, size in models_in_max:
            # Try to move to any other GPU
            for dest in range(gpu_num):
                if dest == max_gpu:
                    continue
                
                if current_sizes[dest] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
                    continue
                
                # Simulate
                new_max_size = current_sizes[max_gpu] - size
                new_max_load = current_loads[max_gpu] - load
                new_dest_size = current_sizes[dest] + size
                new_dest_load = current_loads[dest] + load
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size) if new_max_size > 1e-6 else 0
                new_dest_kvpr = new_dest_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_dest_size)
                
                new_global_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr)
                for g in range(gpu_num):
                    if g != max_gpu and g != dest:
                        new_global_max = max(new_global_max, current_kvprs[g].item())
                
                if new_global_max < max_kvpr * 0.998:  # Only accept significant improvement
                    # Execute move
                    current_assignment[max_gpu].pop(i)
                    current_assignment[dest].append(idx)
                    
                    current_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_size
                    current_loads[max_gpu] = new_max_load
                    current_sizes[dest] = new_dest_size
                    current_loads[dest] = new_dest_load
                    
                    current_kvprs[max_gpu] = new_max_kvpr
                    current_kvprs[dest] = new_dest_kvpr
                    
                    moved = True
                    break
            
            if moved:
                break
        
        if not moved:
            break
    
    # Convert back to Model objects
    final_placement = {g: [] for g in range(gpu_num)}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        for idx in current_assignment[g]:
            original_idx = sorted_indices[idx].item()
            final_placement[g].append(models[original_idx])
    
    return final_placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # PARADIGM SHIFT VARIANT: Enhanced Greedy with Adaptive Thresholding and Dynamic Swap Prioritization
    # Modifications:
    # - Use adaptive memory utilization threshold (not fixed penalty)
    # - Prioritize swaps based on load imbalance reduction, not just KVPR drop
    # - Add early termination with convergence check
    # - Use weighted score combining KVPR, memory utilization, and load imbalance
    # - Improve fallback logic with load-aware placement
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Precompute metrics
    model_loads = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_sizes = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    # Helper to calculate KVPR
    def compute_kvpr(load: float, size: float) -> float:
        if size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            return float('inf')
        return load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - size)
    
    # --- Phase 1: Enhanced Greedy Assignment ---
    # Strategy: Sort models by Load Density (Load / Size) descending.
    # Assign to GPU that minimizes a composite score: KVPR + adaptive memory penalty + load imbalance penalty.
    # The memory penalty scales with current GPU utilization to encourage balanced memory usage.
    
    sorted_indices = sorted(range(n_models), 
                            key=lambda i: model_loads[i] / model_sizes[i] if model_sizes[i] > 0 else float('inf'), 
                            reverse=True)
    
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_loads = [0.0] * gpu_num
    gpu_sizes = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        best_gpu = -1
        best_score = float('inf')
        
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            # Check memory constraint
            if gpu_sizes[g] + model_sizes[idx] > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            # Calculate new KVPR
            new_load = gpu_loads[g] + model_loads[idx]
            new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + model_sizes[idx]
            new_kvpr = compute_kvpr(new_load, new_size)
            
            # Adaptive memory penalty: increases as GPU gets fuller
            utilization = new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE
            memory_penalty = 0.1 * utilization * (1 - utilization)  # Peaks at 50% utilization
            load_imbalance_penalty = 0.05 * abs(new_kvpr - sum(gpu_loads) / gpu_num)  # Penalize deviation from average load
            
            # Composite score: KVPR dominates, but penalties discourage imbalance
            score = new_kvpr + memory_penalty + load_imbalance_penalty
            
            if score < best_score:
                best_score = score
                best_gpu = g
        
        if best_gpu != -1:
            placement[best_gpu].append(idx)
            gpu_loads[best_gpu] += model_loads[idx]
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += model_sizes[idx]
        else:
            # Fallback: place on GPU with most free space, but prefer those with lower load
            # Use a hybrid metric: free space + load balance
            best_gpu = max(range(gpu_num), 
                           key=lambda g: (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g], -gpu_loads[g]))
            placement[best_gpu].append(idx)
            gpu_loads[best_gpu] += model_loads[idx]
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += model_sizes[idx]
    
    # --- Phase 2: Adaptive Local Search with Convergence Control ---
    # Use a more aggressive swap strategy with dynamic thresholds.
    # Track convergence via stability of max KVPR over iterations.
    # Prioritize swaps that reduce the largest KVPR gap and improve load balance.
    
    max_iterations = 300
    last_max_kvpr = float('inf')
    consecutive_no_improvement = 0
    tolerance = 1e-8
    
    for _ in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        current_kvprs = [compute_kvpr(gpu_loads[g], gpu_sizes[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)]
        max_kvpr = max(current_kvprs)
        bottleneck_gpu = current_kvprs.index(max_kvpr)
        
        # Check for convergence
        if abs(max_kvpr - last_max_kvpr) < tolerance:
            consecutive_no_improvement += 1
            if consecutive_no_improvement >= 10:
                break
        else:
            consecutive_no_improvement = 0
            last_max_kvpr = max_kvpr
        
        # Identify target GPUs: those with low KVPR and good free space
        target_gpus = []
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if g == bottleneck_gpu:
                continue
            if current_kvprs[g] < max_kvpr - 1e-5:
                # Weighted score: more free space and lower load are better
                score = (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g]) * 1.0 + (max_kvpr - current_kvprs[g]) * 0.5
                target_gpus.append((g, score))
        
        target_gpus.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
        if not target_gpus:
            break  # No improvement possible
        
        improvement_found = False
        
        # Try direct moves: prioritize models from bottleneck with high load/size ratio
        for target_gpu, _ in target_gpus:
            # Sort models in bottleneck by impact: high load/size helps reduce bottleneck most
            bottleneck_models = placement[bottleneck_gpu][:]
            bottleneck_models.sort(key=lambda i: (model_loads[i] / max(1, model_sizes[i])), reverse=True)
            
            for m_idx in bottleneck_models:
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + model_sizes[m_idx] > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Simulate move
                new_load_b = gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] - model_loads[m_idx]
                new_size_b = gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] - model_sizes[m_idx]
                new_kvpr_b = compute_kvpr(new_load_b, new_size_b)
                
                new_load_t = gpu_loads[target_gpu] + model_loads[m_idx]
                new_size_t = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + model_sizes[m_idx]
                new_kvpr_t = compute_kvpr(new_load_t, new_size_t)
                
                # New max KVPR after move
                new_max = max(new_kvpr_b, new_kvpr_t, max(v for g, v in enumerate(current_kvprs) if g not in [bottleneck_gpu, target_gpu]))
                
                # Accept only if max KVPR decreases significantly
                if new_max < max_kvpr - 1e-7:
                    placement[bottleneck_gpu].remove(m_idx)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(m_idx)
                    
                    gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] = new_load_b
                    gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] = new_size_b
                    gpu_loads[target_gpu] = new_load_t
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_size_t
                    
                    improvement_found = True
                    break
            
            if improvement_found:
                break
        
        # If no direct move, try swaps with best target
        if not improvement_found:
            for target_gpu, _ in target_gpus:
                for m1 in placement[bottleneck_gpu]:
                    for m2 in placement[target_gpu]:
                        # Check memory constraints
                        if (gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] - model_sizes[m1] + model_sizes[m2] > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - model_sizes[m2] + model_sizes[m1] > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Simulate swap
                        new_load_b = gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] - model_loads[m1] + model_loads[m2]
                        new_size_b = gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] - model_sizes[m1] + model_sizes[m2]
                        new_kvpr_b = compute_kvpr(new_load_b, new_size_b)
                        
                        new_load_t = gpu_loads[target_gpu] - model_loads[m2] + model_loads[m1]
                        new_size_t = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - model_sizes[m2] + model_sizes[m1]
                        new_kvpr_t = compute_kvpr(new_load_t, new_size_t)
                        
                        # Calculate new max KVPR
                        new_max = max(new_kvpr_b, new_kvpr_t, max(v for g, v in enumerate(current_kvprs) if g not in [bottleneck_gpu, target_gpu]))
                        
                        if new_max < max_kvpr - 1e-7:
                            placement[bottleneck_gpu].remove(m1)
                            placement[bottleneck_gpu].append(m2)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(m2)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(m1)
                            
                            gpu_loads[bottleneck_gpu] = new_load_b
                            gpu_sizes[bottleneck_gpu] = new_size_b
                            gpu_loads[target_gpu] = new_load_t
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_size_t
                            
                            improvement_found = True
                            break
                    if improvement_found:
                        break
                if improvement_found:
                    break
        
        if not improvement_found:
            break  # No improvement in this round
    
    # Convert indices back to Model objects
    final_result = {}
    for gpu_id, model_indices in placement.items():
        final_result[gpu_id] = [models[i] for i in model_indices]
    
    return final_result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import torch
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Convert to tensors
    n_models = len(models)
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_loads = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Calculate load density (load per GB) - key metric for balancing
    load_density = model_loads / (model_sizes + 1e-8)  # Avoid division by zero
    
    # Sort by load density descending (most "expensive" models first)
    sort_idx = torch.argsort(load_density, descending=True)
    model_sizes = model_sizes[sort_idx]
    model_loads = model_loads[sort_idx]
    
    # Phase 1: Multi-start greedy with different objective functions
    def greedy_with_objective(obj_func):
        """Greedy placement using specified objective function."""
        sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        loads = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        assignment = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
        
        for i in range(n_models):
            size = model_sizes[i].item()
            load = model_loads[i].item()
            
            # Find valid GPUs
            valid_mask = (sizes + size) <= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5  # Slight buffer for floating point
            if not valid_mask.any():
                # Fallback: put on GPU with most space
                target_gpu = torch.argmax(GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes).item()
            else:
                # Calculate objective for each valid GPU
                candidates = torch.where(valid_mask)[0]
                scores = []
                
                for g in candidates:
                    new_size = sizes[g] + size
                    new_load = loads[g] + load
                    kvpr = new_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                    scores.append(obj_func(g, new_size, new_load, kvpr, sizes, loads))
                
                best_idx = torch.argmin(torch.tensor(scores)).item()
                target_gpu = candidates[best_idx].item()
            
            assignment[target_gpu].append(i)
            sizes[target_gpu] += size
            loads[target_gpu] += load
        
        return assignment, sizes, loads
    
    # Define different objective functions with adjusted weights
    def obj_kvpr_only(g, new_size, new_load, kvpr, sizes, loads):
        return kvpr
    
    def obj_with_space(g, new_size, new_load, kvpr, sizes, loads):
        space_util = new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE
        space_penalty = (space_util - 0.5) ** 2  # Penalize deviation from 50% utilization
        return kvpr + space_penalty * 0.05
    
    def obj_with_balance(g, new_size, new_load, kvpr, sizes, loads):
        avg_load = (loads.sum() + new_load) / gpu_num
        current_gpu_load = loads[g] + new_load
        imbalance = abs(current_gpu_load - avg_load)
        return kvpr + imbalance * 0.02
    
    def obj_combined(g, new_size, new_load, kvpr, sizes, loads):
        space_util = new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE
        space_penalty = (space_util - 0.6) ** 2  # Shift target to 60% utilization
        avg_load = (loads.sum() + new_load) / gpu_num
        imbalance = abs((loads[g] + new_load) - avg_load)
        return kvpr * 1.8 + space_penalty * 0.6 + imbalance * 0.04
    
    # Try all heuristics and keep best
    best_assignment = None
    best_max_kvpr = float('inf')
    
    for obj_func in [obj_kvpr_only, obj_with_space, obj_with_balance, obj_combined]:
        assignment, sizes, loads = greedy_with_objective(obj_func)
        kvprs = loads / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes)
        max_kvpr = kvprs.max().item()
        
        if max_kvpr < best_max_kvpr:
            best_max_kvpr = max_kvpr
            best_assignment = assignment
    
    # Phase 2: Adaptive local search with smart moves
    gpu_assignments = [list(best_assignment[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Helper to get state
    def get_state(assignment):
        sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        loads = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            for idx in assignment[g]:
                sizes[g] += model_sizes[idx]
                loads[g] += model_loads[idx]
        return sizes, loads
    
    sizes, loads = get_state(gpu_assignments)
    kvprs = loads / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sizes)
    
    # Adaptive search loop
    max_iter = 2500
    patience = 180
    no_improve = 0
    
    for iteration in range(max_iter):
        current_max = kvprs.max().item()
        
        # Strategy 1: Move from highest to lowest KVPR
        source = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        dest = torch.argmin(kvprs).item()
        
        if source == dest:
            break
        
        # Try to find best model to move
        best_move = None
        best_improvement = 0
        
        for idx in gpu_assignments[source]:
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            load = model_loads[idx].item()
            
            if sizes[dest] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
                continue
            
            # Calculate improvement
            new_source_size = sizes[source] - size
            new_source_load = loads[source] - load
            new_dest_size = sizes[dest] + size
            new_dest_load = loads[dest] + load
            
            new_source_kvpr = new_source_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_source_size) if new_source_size < GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5 else 0
            new_dest_kvpr = new_dest_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_dest_size)
            
            # Calculate new max considering all GPUs
            new_max = max(new_source_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr)
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if g != source and g != dest:
                    new_max = max(new_max, kvprs[g])
            
            improvement = current_max - new_max
            
            # Prefer moves that reduce max KVPR, but also consider load balancing
            if improvement > 0 and improvement > best_improvement:
                best_improvement = improvement
                best_move = (idx, size, load, new_source_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr)
        
        if best_move:
            idx, size, load, new_source_kvpr, new_dest_kvpr = best_move
            gpu_assignments[source].remove(idx)
            gpu_assignments[dest].append(idx)
            
            sizes[source] -= size
            loads[source] -= load
            sizes[dest] += size
            loads[dest] += load
            
            kvprs[source] = new_source_kvpr
            kvprs[dest] = new_dest_kvpr
            
            no_improve = 0
        else:
            no_improve += 1
            
            # Strategy 2: Try pairwise swaps if no improvement
            if no_improve > 60:
                improved = False
                
                for g1 in range(gpu_num):
                    for g2 in range(g1 + 1, gpu_num):
                        if not gpu_assignments[g1] or not gpu_assignments[g2]:
                            continue
                        
                        # Try swapping each pair
                        for idx1 in gpu_assignments[g1]:
                            for idx2 in gpu_assignments[g2]:
                                size1 = model_sizes[idx1].item()
                                load1 = model_loads[idx1].item()
                                size2 = model_sizes[idx2].item()
                                load2 = model_loads[idx2].item()
                                
                                # Check feasibility
                                if (sizes[g1] - size1 + size2 > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5 or
                                    sizes[g2] - size2 + size1 > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5):
                                    continue
                                
                                # Simulate swap
                                new_size1 = sizes[g1] - size1 + size2
                                new_load1 = loads[g1] - load1 + load2
                                new_size2 = sizes[g2] - size2 + size1
                                new_load2 = loads[g2] - load2 + load1
                                
                                new_kvpr1 = new_load1 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size1)
                                new_kvpr2 = new_load2 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size2)
                                
                                new_max = max(new_kvpr1, new_kvpr2)
                                for g in range(gpu_num):
                                    if g != g1 and g != g2:
                                        new_max = max(new_max, kvprs[g])
                                
                                if new_max < current_max:
                                    # Execute swap
                                    gpu_assignments[g1].remove(idx1)
                                    gpu_assignments[g1].append(idx2)
                                    gpu_assignments[g2].remove(idx2)
                                    gpu_assignments[g2].append(idx1)
                                    
                                    sizes[g1] = new_size1
                                    loads[g1] = new_load1
                                    sizes[g2] = new_size2
                                    loads[g2] = new_load2
                                    kvprs[g1] = new_kvpr1
                                    kvprs[g2] = new_kvpr2
                                    
                                    improved = True
                                    no_improve = 0
                                    break
                            if improved:
                                break
                        if improved:
                            break
                    if improved:
                        break
                
                if not improved:
                    break
    
    # Phase 3: Final refinement - try random moves to escape local minima
    for _ in range(150):
        g1 = torch.randint(0, gpu_num, (1,)).item()
        g2 = torch.randint(0, gpu_num, (1,)).item()
        
        if g1 == g2 or not gpu_assignments[g1]:
            continue
        
        # Try moving a random model from g1 to g2
        idx = gpu_assignments[g1][torch.randint(0, len(gpu_assignments[g1]), (1,)).item()]
        size = model_sizes[idx].item()
        load = model_loads[idx].item()
        
        if sizes[g2] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5:
            continue
        
        # Check improvement
        new_size1 = sizes[g1] - size
        new_load1 = loads[g1] - load
        new_size2 = sizes[g2] + size
        new_load2 = loads[g2] + load
        
        new_kvpr1 = new_load1 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size1) if new_size1 < GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-5 else 0
        new_kvpr2 = new_load2 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size2)
        
        new_max = max(new_kvpr1, new_kvpr2)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if g != g1 and g != g2:
                new_max = max(new_max, kvprs[g])
        
        if new_max < kvprs.max().item():
            gpu_assignments[g1].remove(idx)
            gpu_assignments[g2].append(idx)
            
            sizes[g1] = new_size1
            loads[g1] = new_load1
            sizes[g2] = new_size2
            loads[g2] = new_load2
            kvprs[g1] = new_kvpr1
            kvprs[g2] = new_kvpr2
    
    # Convert back to original model objects
    final_placement = {g: [] for g in range(gpu_num)}
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        for idx in gpu_assignments[g]:
            original_idx = sort_idx[idx].item()
            final_placement[g].append(models[original_idx])
    
    return final_placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import torch
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Variant: Enhanced Binary Search with Adaptive Heuristics and Improved Edge Handling
    # Modifications:
    # - Increased precision in binary search via dynamic epsilon
    # - Replaced "min remaining capacity" with "max slack + space" heuristic
    # - Added early termination for degenerate cases
    # - Improved swap strategy with load-size ratio prioritization
    # - Modified fallback behavior for edge cases

    if not models:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Precompute metrics
    n_models = len(models)
    sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    loads = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float64)
    
    # Sort models by size descending, then by load descending (primary for bin packing)
    sorted_indices = sorted(range(n_models), key=lambda i: (sizes[i], loads[i]), reverse=True)
    
    # 1. Compute tighter lower bounds
    total_size = sizes.sum().item()
    total_load = loads.sum().item()
    
    # Individual lower bound: max load / (80 - size) for each model
    individual_bounds = []
    for i in range(n_models):
        if 80 - sizes[i].item() > 1e-6:
            individual_bounds.append(loads[i].item() / (80 - sizes[i].item()))
    min_individual = max(individual_bounds) if individual_bounds else 0.0
    
    # Aggregate lower bound: total_load / (gpu_num * 80 - total_size)
    if gpu_num * 80 - total_size > 1e-6:
        agg_bound = total_load / (gpu_num * 80 - total_size)
    else:
        agg_bound = 0.0
    
    low = max(min_individual, agg_bound)
    
    # Upper bound: Greedy placement with KVPR minimization
    def get_greedy_max_kvpr():
        gpu_loads = [0.0] * gpu_num
        gpu_sizes = [0.0] * gpu_num
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            best_gpu = -1
            best_score = float('inf')
            
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[g] + sizes[idx].item() > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
                    continue
                
                new_load = gpu_loads[g] + loads[idx].item()
                new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + sizes[idx].item()
                kvpr = new_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                
                # Tie-breaker: prioritize GPUs with higher remaining space
                if kvpr < best_score or (abs(kvpr - best_score) < 1e-9 and new_size > gpu_sizes[best_gpu]):
                    best_score = kvpr
                    best_gpu = g
            
            if best_gpu != -1:
                gpu_loads[best_gpu] += loads[idx].item()
                gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += sizes[idx].item()
            else:
                return float('inf')
        
        return max(gpu_loads[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g]) for g in range(gpu_num))
    
    high = get_greedy_max_kvpr()
    
    # 2. Binary Search with Adaptive Epsilon and Robust Feasibility Check
    EPS = 1e-8
    best_placement = None
    optimal_kvpr = high
    
    # Pre-allocate for performance
    gpu_loads = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float64)
    gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float64)
    
    def is_feasible(kvp_limit):
        gpu_loads.zero_()
        gpu_sizes.zero_()
        temp_placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            s = sizes[idx].item()
            l = loads[idx].item()
            
            candidates = []
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[g] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
                    continue
                
                # Constraint: (load + l) / (80 - size - s) <= kvp_limit
                # => load + l <= kvp_limit * (80 - size - s)
                # => load + l + kvp_limit * s <= kvp_limit * (80 - size)
                lhs = gpu_loads[g] + l + kvp_limit * s
                rhs = kvp_limit * (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                
                if lhs <= rhs + EPS:
                    # Use combined score: (slack + space_left) to favor balanced, underutilized GPUs
                    slack = rhs - lhs
                    space_left = GPU_MEM_SIZE - (gpu_sizes[g] + s)
                    score = slack + space_left
                    candidates.append((g, score))
            
            if not candidates:
                return False, None
            
            # Choose GPU with highest combined score
            best_g = max(candidates, key=lambda x: x[1])[0]
            temp_placement[best_g].append(idx)
            gpu_loads[best_g] += l
            gpu_sizes[best_g] += s
        
        return True, temp_placement

    # Binary search with adaptive precision
    for iter in range(70):  # Slightly more iterations for higher precision
        mid = (low + high) / 2.0
        
        # Use adaptive epsilon based on current range
        adaptive_eps = max(1e-9, (high - low) * 1e-6)
        
        feasible, placement = is_feasible(mid)
        
        if feasible:
            optimal_kvpr = mid
            best_placement = placement
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
        
        # Early termination if convergence is achieved
        if high - low < adaptive_eps:
            break
    
    # Fallback to greedy if no valid placement found
    if best_placement is None:
        gpu_loads = [0.0] * gpu_num
        gpu_sizes = [0.0] * gpu_num
        best_placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            best_gpu = -1
            best_score = float('inf')
            
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[g] + sizes[idx].item() > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
                    continue
                
                new_load = gpu_loads[g] + loads[idx].item()
                new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + sizes[idx].item()
                kvpr = new_load / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                
                if kvpr < best_score:
                    best_score = kvpr
                    best_gpu = g
            
            if best_gpu != -1:
                best_placement[best_gpu].append(idx)
                gpu_loads[best_gpu] += loads[idx].item()
                gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += sizes[idx].item()
    
    # 3. Post-Processing: Enhanced Local Refinement with Dynamic Strategy Switching
    # Now refine the placement using a multi-stage improvement strategy
    
    current_placement = {k: v[:] for k, v in best_placement.items()}
    gpu_loads_f = torch.tensor([sum(loads[i].item() for i in current_placement[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)], dtype=torch.float64)
    gpu_sizes_f = torch.tensor([sum(sizes[i].item() for i in current_placement[g]) for g in range(gpu_num)], dtype=torch.float64)
    
    def get_kvpr(g):
        size = gpu_sizes_f[g].item()
        if size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
            return float('inf')
        return gpu_loads_f[g].item() / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - size)
    
    # Refinement loop with dynamic strategy
    for _ in range(400):
        kvprs = torch.tensor([get_kvpr(g) for g in range(gpu_num)])
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        min_gpu = torch.argmin(kvprs).item()
        
        # Early exit if already balanced
        if kvprs[max_gpu] - kvprs[min_gpu] < 1e-7:
            break
        
        improved = False
        
        # Strategy 1: Move high-impact models (high load/size) from max GPU to min GPU
        if current_placement[max_gpu]:
            # Sort by load/size ratio descending (high impact first)
            candidates = sorted(current_placement[max_gpu], 
                                key=lambda i: (loads[i].item() / (sizes[i].item() + 1e-6)), reverse=True)
            
            for idx in candidates:
                s = sizes[idx].item()
                l = loads[idx].item()
                
                # Check memory
                if gpu_sizes_f[min_gpu].item() + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6:
                    continue
                
                # Compute new KVPRs
                new_load_max = gpu_loads_f[max_gpu].item() - l
                new_size_max = gpu_sizes_f[max_gpu].item() - s
                new_kvpr_max = new_load_max / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size_max) if new_size_max < GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6 else float('inf')
                
                new_load_min = gpu_loads_f[min_gpu].item() + l
                new_size_min = gpu_sizes_f[min_gpu].item() + s
                new_kvpr_min = new_load_min / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size_min)
                
                new_max = max(new_kvpr_max, new_kvpr_min)
                old_max = kvprs[max_gpu].item()
                
                if new_max < old_max - 1e-7:
                    # Apply move
                    current_placement[max_gpu].remove(idx)
                    current_placement[min_gpu].append(idx)
                    gpu_loads_f[max_gpu] = new_load_max
                    gpu_sizes_f[max_gpu] = new_size_max
                    gpu_loads_f[min_gpu] = new_load_min
                    gpu_sizes_f[min_gpu] = new_size_min
                    improved = True
                    break
            
            if improved:
                continue
        
        # Strategy 2: Swap between max and other GPUs using a more balanced criterion
        # Prioritize swaps that reduce the max KVPR by the largest margin
        target_gpus = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: kvprs[g], reverse=True)
        
        for g1 in [max_gpu]:
            for g2 in target_gpus:
                if g1 == g2 or not current_placement[g1] or not current_placement[g2]:
                    continue
                
                # Sort models by load/size ratio
                # g1: high ratio → move out
                # g2: low ratio → move in
                sorted_g1 = sorted(current_placement[g1], key=lambda i: (loads[i].item() / (sizes[i].item() + 1e-6)), reverse=True)
                sorted_g2 = sorted(current_placement[g2], key=lambda i: (loads[i].item() / (sizes[i].item() + 1e-6)))
                
                # Try top candidates
                for m1 in sorted_g1[:min(3, len(sorted_g1))]:
                    for m2 in sorted_g2[:min(3, len(sorted_g2))]:
                        s1, s2 = sizes[m1].item(), sizes[m2].item()
                        l1, l2 = loads[m1].item(), loads[m2].item()
                        
                        # Check memory
                        if (gpu_sizes_f[g1].item() - s1 + s2 > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6 or
                            gpu_sizes_f[g2].item() - s2 + s1 > GPU_MEM_SIZE - 1e-6):
                            continue
                        
                        # New KVPRs
                        new_load1 = gpu_loads_f[g1].item() - l1 + l2
                        new_size1 = gpu_sizes_f[g1].item() - s1 + s2
                        new_kvpr1 = new_load1 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size1)
                        
                        new_load2 = gpu_loads_f[g2].item() - l2 + l1
                        new_size2 = gpu_sizes_f[g2].item() - s2 + s1
                        new_kvpr2 = new_load2 / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size2)
                        
                        new_max = max(new_kvpr1, new_kvpr2)
                        old_max = max(kvprs[g1].item(), kvprs[g2].item())
                        
                        if new_max < old_max - 1e-7:
                            # Apply swap
                            current_placement[g1].remove(m1)
                            current_placement[g1].append(m2)
                            current_placement[g2].remove(m2)
                            current_placement[g2].append(m1)
                            
                            gpu_loads_f[g1] = new_load1
                            gpu_sizes_f[g1] = new_size1
                            gpu_loads_f[g2] = new_load2
                            gpu_sizes_f[g2] = new_size2
                            improved = True
                            break
                    if improved:
                        break
                if improved:
                    break
            
            if improved:
                continue
        
        if not improved:
            break

    # Final conversion to output format
    result = {}
    for gpu_id, model_indices in current_placement.items():
        result[gpu_id] = [models[i] for i in model_indices]
    
    return result

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
import heapq
import math

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute load factor for each model
    model_loads = [(m, m.req_rate / m.slo) for m in models]
    
    # Sort models by load factor descending, then by size ascending (to avoid overloading small GPUs)
    model_loads.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1], -x[0].model_size), reverse=True)
    
    # Initialize GPU states: remaining memory, current load sum, list of models
    gpu_states = [{'mem': GPU_MEM_SIZE, 'load': 0.0, 'models': []} for _ in range(gpu_num)]
    
    # Track current max KVPR
    current_max_kvpr = 0.0
    
    # Place models one by one
    for model, load in model_loads:
        best_gpu_idx = -1
        best_new_kvpr = float('inf')
        
        # Evaluate each GPU
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            state = gpu_states[gpu_id]
            
            # Check memory constraint
            if state['mem'] < model.model_size:
                continue
            
            # Calculate new KVPR if placed here
            new_mem = state['mem'] - model.model_size
            new_load = state['load'] + load
            new_kvpr = new_load / new_mem if new_mem > 0 else float('inf')
            
            # Memory pressure penalty: more aggressive thresholds for higher utilization
            utilization = (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_mem) / GPU_MEM_SIZE
            memory_pressure = 0.0
            if utilization > 0.92:
                memory_pressure = 1.2
            elif utilization > 0.85:
                memory_pressure = 0.8
            elif utilization > 0.75:
                memory_pressure = 0.4
            else:
                memory_pressure = 0.0
            
            # Load imbalance penalty: increased sensitivity with dynamic base
            total_load = sum(s['load'] for s in gpu_states) + load
            avg_load = total_load / gpu_num
            load_imbalance = abs(new_load - avg_load) * 0.15  # Slightly higher weight
            
            # Entropy term: promote even distribution with stronger diversity emphasis
            model_count = sum(len(s['models']) for s in gpu_states)
            avg_models_per_gpu = model_count / gpu_num
            diversity_score = 1.0 - abs(len(gpu_states[gpu_id]['models']) - avg_models_per_gpu) / (avg_models_per_gpu + 1e-6)
            entropy_penalty = 0.008 * diversity_score  # Increased weight for diversity
            
            # Add small jitter to break ties, now using GPU ID + model size for randomness
            jitter = 1e-6 * (gpu_id * 100 + model.model_size % 100)
            
            # Combine all components with tuned weights
            adjusted_kvpr = new_kvpr + memory_pressure + load_imbalance + entropy_penalty + jitter
            
            if adjusted_kvpr < best_new_kvpr:
                best_new_kvpr = adjusted_kvpr
                best_gpu_idx = gpu_id
        
        # Fallback: if no GPU found (shouldn't happen with valid inputs), 
        # place on GPU with most remaining memory
        if best_gpu_idx == -1:
            best_gpu_idx = max(range(gpu_num), key=lambda g: gpu_states[g]['mem'])
        
        # Update GPU state
        gpu_states[best_gpu_idx]['mem'] -= model.model_size
        gpu_states[best_gpu_idx]['load'] += load
        gpu_states[best_gpu_idx]['models'].append(model)
        
        # Update current max KVPR
        current_max_kvpr = max(current_max_kvpr, 
                              gpu_states[best_gpu_idx]['load'] / gpu_states[best_gpu_idx]['mem'])
    
    # Phase 2: Refinement using local search
    # We'll try to move models from high-KVPR GPUs to low-KVPR GPUs
    
    # Calculate KVPR for each GPU
    def get_gpu_kvpr(gpu_idx):
        state = gpu_states[gpu_idx]
        if not state['models'] or state['mem'] >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            return 0.0
        return state['load'] / state['mem']
    
    # Local search: try moving models from highest KVPR to lowest KVPR
    max_iterations = 2000  # Slightly increased for more refinement
    for _ in range(max_iterations):
        # Get current KVPRs
        kvprs = [(get_gpu_kvpr(i), i) for i in range(gpu_num)]
        kvprs.sort(reverse=True)
        
        highest_kvpr, highest_idx = kvprs[0]
        lowest_kvpr, lowest_idx = kvprs[-1]
        
        # If no significant difference, stop
        if highest_kvpr - lowest_kvpr < 0.0001:
            break
        
        # Try to move a model from highest to lowest
        source_state = gpu_states[highest_idx]
        dest_state = gpu_states[lowest_idx]
        
        if not source_state['models']:
            break
        
        # Sort models in source GPU by load factor descending, then by size ascending (favor smaller models for mobility)
        source_models_with_load = [(m, m.req_rate / m.slo) for m in source_state['models']]
        source_models_with_load.sort(key=lambda x: (-x[1], x[0].model_size))
        
        moved = False
        for model, load in source_models_with_load:
            # Check if move is valid
            if model.model_size <= dest_state['mem']:
                # Simulate move
                new_source_load = source_state['load'] - load
                new_source_mem = source_state['mem'] + model.model_size
                new_dest_load = dest_state['load'] + load
                new_dest_mem = dest_state['mem'] - model.model_size
                
                if new_dest_mem <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_source_kvpr = new_source_load / new_source_mem if new_source_mem < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 0.0
                new_dest_kvpr = new_dest_load / new_dest_mem
                
                # Check if this reduces the max KVPR
